title: What is the best CRM workflow for multiple Products for a single Deal item?
excerpt: I’m trying to figure out a good workflow, whether that be products referenced in a connection board itself or in the sub-item columns and instead mirrored back to the main item instead? Wondering if anyone has run into t…

posts.author: JFergus (Joseph Fergus)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m trying to figure out a good workflow, whether that be products referenced in a connection board itself or in the sub-item columns and instead mirrored back to the main item instead? Wondering if anyone has run into the same issue and found a good pipeline workflow.

We have multiple products we sell and sometime will sell them at the same time for an order/quote or we are looking with the client on the best option (if they want a subscription plan or perpetual plan). Whether that be a specific tier (individual product/pro product) or billing cycle plan type (perpetual product /subscription product), same product but some qualify for special discounts (one falls within a retro-pricing discount while the other is new full price) or separate product types entirely (product 1/product 2).

Subitems seemed the most logical but not only does it take forever to load subitems, there are some limitations with sub-items what is can do. If I use the main item columns though, I can’t separate the items and their discounts and only solution is to apply the whole discount all together and thus limits my ability to see things like what quantities and discounts of each item we did with specific customers. Instead it’s just a general quantity and general discount.

Currently the only suggestion I can think of is if we want to see line items and their individual discounts is to go directly to our invoicing software and pull reports for each customer but because we work with resellers we are not getting accurate data on the customer. So I’d rather we have ability to see reports on each customer though our CRM with ease for cross-selling and up-selling opportunities.

Looking for anyone that has a similar setup for their CRM and has found an effective way to tackle deals with multiple line item products and what their solution was? I know some softwares like Saleforce had this ability that was all integrated in the Opportunity’s board from a previous company but wondering of there is a good workaround for MondayCRM?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I use workflow and have the team fill in counts on the main item (each product is a column), then the workflow creates each of those items as a subitem with the ‘normal’ pricing. The team can then edit those subitems / adjust pricing as needed. It’s not the best option but it works until I can find something better

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re running into a major limitation with monday CRM that might be a dealbreaker for us.

In “another” popular CRM, there’s a designated area where you can add products as a structured table — product names, quantities, prices, etc. It’s clear, visible, and integrated.

In monday, there’s no real equivalent. The Quotes & Invoices feature is a very lightweight version, but the problem is:

  • Once a quote is created, it just “sits” there in the quote.

  • To view it, I have to click into it, then download it — which is slow and cumbersome.

  • More importantly, the quote isn’t treated like a file column or structured data, which means:

    • I can’t use it in automations or formulas

    • I can’t integrate it easily with 3rd party apps (e.g., sending for signature via GetSign, PandaDoc, etc.).

This lack of flexibility makes the Quotes & Invoices feature feel disconnected from the rest of monday’s workflow power. For teams that rely heavily on quoting and contracts, this gap could be a dealbreaker for using monday CRM long term.

Is anyone else running into this? And is there a roadmap to make Quotes & Invoices behave more like a true file column (or expose its data for automations/integrations)?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
We use workflows and automations to get it to where we want, but we also don’t use the quotes and invoices tool. I did meet with one the product owners of the Q&I recently and gave him the feedback, he seemed open to it.

In the meantime, I have a products board that the workflow pulls from, adds the product as a subitem, pulling in the price and then formula columns to look at that & QTY to get total price. Then we use Docugen to make a ‘pretty quote’

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: madebyfell (Tiera)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an interesting workflow! Are you willing to share more details on how your workflow works? How does it add the products as a subitem? Are you able to display the subitems as lineitems in the quote/invoice you make with Docugen?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes and yes! I’ll try to put together some visuals later tonight and share

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: JFergus (Joseph Fergus)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is a new workflow by Monday now in BETA to allow you to add multiple products in the Widget view on the deal board but you do need to have a product board as well for this. If you don’t see this option, you may have to request to get it enabled.

Go to your deal board, then go to a deal item, select “open deal page“ of that item. Then go to the “…“ in the top right and select “edit layout and content” then select select “Add Widget“ then “product“ widget.

It’s still in early beta form but this is extremely helpful. There is more I wish it could do but it does like 90% of what we need… hard part now is figuring out how to copy previous subitem data and copying the data over. If we could get a few more tweaks to this, it could entirely replace our subitem and we could remove subitem all together. Mainly biggest issue is how it ties into the internal quotes and invoice feature they also have. For now I don’t see a way to reference this data for some reports and charts as well as the total is “the total“ and not an option to run as a subtotal as we then have future discounting with resellers after the subtotal like we do in quickbooks that if we could somehow integrate that part. If we could fix the reporting as well as the Quotes and Invoice from products to factor in resellers with both the reseller % discount as well as in the quote and invoice choose the bill to and ship to to be two separate fields, this would be a huge weight lifted off our shoulders.

posts.author: B-H (Boris)
posts.cookedHtml:
This sounds very interesting. We are testing Monday CRM as well and that’s what is currently missing for us as well.
Some way to simply have a deal you can add the products you have defined and the deal value is automatically calculated. Ideally also with a value manually defined as some items are not always listed products such as one time item or indvidual cost for each project, but for the evalucation in the dashboards it would be great to see the deal values accumulated by product to identify good running products etc.
Right now I can only have a products board and a deal board and link them in the way that I can see what products for the deal but not the granularity or automation of the deal value based on the product selection as well as the evaluation in the dashboards.
I wonder why this wasn’t implemented years ago as it would be a no brainer for me.
Ideally having a warehouse connection for the number of items in storage. Any sales would love to know if he can ship that many directly and it could be linked to Shopify etc.
